county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Buena Vista County formed 1851 from Sac. County seat is Storm Lake.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Storm Lake holds deeds from 1858; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Buena Vista County was organized in 1858 and attracted Scandinavian settlers in the 1870s-1880s. Storm Lake later became home to Buena Vista University (est. 1891). In recent decades, meatpacking plants drew Laotian, Latino, and other immigrant communities. Early settler records focus on railroad land purchases. Buena Vista County Historical Society. BLM GLO patents online. FamilySearch microfilm. Sioux Rapids and Newell communities have separate historical collections.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Buena Vista County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Buena Vista County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Storm Lake
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1851 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Buena Vista County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Storm Lake
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Buena Vista County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Storm Lake / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1851. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Buena Vista County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Buena Vista County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Buena Vista County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Buena Vista County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Buena Vista County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Buena Vista County Auditor, Storm Lake
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Buena Vista County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.repository: Luther College Archives, Decorah / ELCA Archives / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Norwegian and Swedish Lutheran church records for Buena Vista County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, burials. Many Norwegian Synod and Hauge Synod congregations.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Butler County formed 1851 from Buchanan, Black Hawk. County seat is Allison.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Allison holds deeds from 1854; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Butler County attracted German, English, and Scandinavian settlers in the 1850s-1860s. Shell Rock and Clarksville communities along the Shell Rock River were early settlement nodes. Butler County Historical Society in Allison. Aplington and Parkersburg area German Evangelical and Reformed church records. Strong agricultural community with farm journals and Agricultural Census schedules at State Archives. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Butler County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Calhoun County formed 1851 from Fox (renamed 1853). County seat is Rockwell City.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Rockwell City holds deeds from 1856; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Originally named Fox County (1851), renamed Calhoun in 1853. Settled primarily in the 1870s-1880s after railroad expansion. Lake City and Lohrville communities. Calhoun County Historical Society. Twin Lakes area attracted sportsmen and resort communities. German and Scandinavian settlers dominate early records. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online. North Raccoon River valley agricultural records at Iowa State Archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Calhoun County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Carroll County formed 1851 from Guthrie. County seat is Carroll.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Carroll holds deeds from 1856; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Carroll County was settled predominantly by Irish Catholic and German Catholic immigrants in the 1860s-1870s. St. Lawrence Catholic Church records in Carroll date from 1869. Kuemper Catholic School records. Carroll County Historical Society. The Manning area has strong German heritage -- Manning Hausbarn Heritage Park preserves a German timber-frame barn. Coon Rapids community records. Raccoon River valley settlement. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Carroll County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cass County formed 1851 from Pottawattamie. County seat is Atlantic.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Atlantic holds deeds from 1853; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Cass County was a parent county for several western Iowa counties. Atlantic was a division point on the Chicago, Rock Island and Pacific Railroad -- railroad employment records. Cass County Historical Society and Cass County Museum in Atlantic. Danish settlers in the Brayton-Wiota area. Griswold and Lewis communities have separate historical collections. East Nishnabotna River valley settlers. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Cass County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cedar County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Tipton.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Tipton holds deeds from 1840; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Cedar County is one of the earliest organized counties from the 1837 Black Hawk Purchase. Strong New England and Pennsylvania Quaker settlement. Springdale Friends Meeting was part of the Underground Railroad network -- John Brown stayed in Springdale before the Harpers Ferry raid. Quaker meeting records at Iowa Yearly Meeting archives. Cedar County Historical Society in Tipton. Mechanicsville and Clarence communities. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Cedar County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cerro Gordo County formed 1851 from Floyd. County seat is Mason City.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Mason City holds deeds from 1856; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Cerro Gordo County is centered on Mason City, an industrial city known for brick and tile manufacturing and meatpacking. Decker Meatpacking and Lehigh Cement employment records document workers. Mason City Public Library has extensive local history collections. Frank Lloyd Wright's Park Inn Hotel and Stockman House reflect the city's architectural heritage. Cerro Gordo County Historical Society. Clear Lake resort community. Strong Norwegian and German settlement. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Cerro Gordo County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cherokee County formed 1851 from Crawford. County seat is Cherokee.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Cherokee holds deeds from 1858; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Cherokee County was organized in 1857 and settled primarily in the 1870s. Cherokee Mental Health Institute (est. 1902) institutional records may contain patient information. The Cherokee area attracted German and Scandinavian settlers. Cherokee County Historical Society. Aurelia and Marcus communities. Little Sioux River valley settlement. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Chickasaw County formed 1851 from Fayette. County seat is New Hampton.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in New Hampton holds deeds from 1853; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Chickasaw County was settled by Irish and Bohemian (Czech) immigrants in the 1850s-1870s. New Hampton was a railroad town. Czech Catholic church records (ZCBJ lodges) in Spillville and surrounding areas are important -- Dvorak visited Spillville in 1893. Chickasaw County Historical Society. Nashua and Ionia communities. Little Cedar River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Chickasaw County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Chickasaw County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Chickasaw County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Chickasaw County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Chickasaw County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Chickasaw County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Chickasaw County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Chickasaw County Auditor, New Hampton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Chickasaw County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Clarke County formed 1846 from Lucas. County seat is Osceola.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Osceola holds deeds from 1851; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Clarke County was settled by migrants from Indiana, Ohio, and the upper South in the late 1840s. Osceola was a stop on the Burlington and Missouri River Railroad. Mormon Trail passed through the county -- Latter-day Saint emigrant records may reference the area. Clarke County Historical Society. Weldon and Murray communities. BLM GLO patents online. FamilySearch microfilm. South River valley agricultural records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Clarke County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Clarke County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Osceola
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1846 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Clarke County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Osceola
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1846. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Clarke County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Osceola / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1846. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Clarke County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Clarke County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Clarke County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1850-1950 for Clarke County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Clarke County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Clarke County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Clarke County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Clarke County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Clarke County Auditor, Osceola
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Clarke County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Mormon Trail Records -- Clarke County Area
source_profiles.repository: LDS Church History Library, Salt Lake City / State Historical Society of Iowa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Latter-day Saint emigrant records, pioneer journals, and camp records for the Clarke County area of the Mormon Trail across Iowa (1846-1869). Includes both LDS members and non-Mormon settlers who arrived along the same corridor.
